arts.ttl: Tuttosport: Ultras leader’s alleged communication with Inter legend Materazzi revealed
arts.sum: A new development has emerged in the ongoing investigation into the ultras groups of Inter Milan and AC Milan. According to reports from Tuttosport, Marco Ferdico, a prominent Inter ultras leader who was recently arrested, had a phone conversation with former Inter defender Marco Materazzi regarding police activities. The investigation, which has led to 19 arrests and effectively dismantled the leadership of both Milan clubs’ ultras groups, continues to uncover complex relationships between the fan organizations and club officials. Massimiliano Silva, an Inter executive responsible for fan relations, has provided a statement to investigators. arts.ttl: Inter eyeing defender Jonathan Tah on free transfer
arts.sum: Bayer Leverkusen defender Jonathan Tah has become a hot topic in the transfer market as his contract nears its end. The 27-year-old German international has confirmed he won’t be renewing with the Bundesliga club, sparking interest from several top European teams. Calciomercato report that Inter Milan has been closely monitoring Tah’s situation for some time. The Nerazzurri are looking to bolster their defensive options, potentially as replacements for aging center-backs Francesco Acerbi and Stefan de Vrij. Tah’s potential availability on a free transfer makes him an attractive prospect for Inter, who are always on the lookout for cost-effective signings. arts.ttl: Wiretap reveals Ultras leader’s ticket request to Inter coach Inzaghi
arts.sum: A recent investigation into Italian football ultras has uncovered a revealing phone conversation between Inter Milan’s head coach Simone Inzaghi and Marco Ferdico, a prominent ultras leader who was recently arrested. La Gazzetta dello Sport report that the discussion, which took place in May 2023, centered around obtaining additional tickets for the Champions League final in Istanbul. According to the intercepted call, Ferdico explicitly asked Inzaghi to intervene with the club’s management, specifically mentioning CEO Beppe Marotta, to secure 200 extra tickets for the ultras group. Inzaghi reportedly promised to intercede with the club’s top executives on their behalf.
